chapter_title.ar: باب مَنْ أَوْصَى بِعِتْقٍ أَوْ صَدَقَةٍ أَوْ حَجٍّ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 13 – One who bequeaths with the libration of a slave, or charity, or Hajj

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (23/28)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (23/28)
text: فِي رَجُلٍ أَوْصَى بِأَكْثَرَ مِنَ الثُّلُثِ وَ أَعْتَقَ مَمْلُوكَهُ فِي مَرَضِهِ فَقَالَ إِنْ كَانَ أَكْثَرَ مِنَ الثُّلُثِ رُدَّ إِلَى الثُّلُثِ وَ جَازَ الْعِتْقُ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from a man, from Muhammad Bin Muslim,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> having said regarding a man who bequeathed more than the third, and emancipated (liberated) his slave during his illness. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘If it was more than the third, return it to the third; and the emancipation is Permissible’.
en.sarwar: 1. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Ali ibn al-Nu’man from Muhammad ibn Muslim who has narrated the following: “About the case of a man who makes a will about more than one-third of his legacy, or setting free a slave during his illness, abu Ja‘far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘If it is more than one-third it is returned to one-third and setting free of slave is permissible/”
